title: Join Us for International World Refugee Day – June 20, 2026
location: Islamic Association of North Texas (IANT), 840 Abrams Rd, Richardson, TX 75081
description: Engineeer4Humanity is honored to be invited once again to the International World Refugee Day Celebration, taking place on Saturday, June 20, 2026, from 10:00 AM to 2:00 PM at the Islamic Association of North Texas (IANT), 840 Abrams Rd, Richardson, TX 75081. Visit www.dallaswrd.com for full event details. This annual gathering—organized in partnership with the City of Dallas, UNHCR, and leading resettlement agencies—celebrates the resilience, contributions, and stories of refugees across our communities.
paragraphs: Following our meaningful participation in the 2024 and 2025 Dallas World Refugee Day events, we are proud to return and stand alongside refugees, advocates, and partners committed to dignity, opportunity, and sustainable peace.

This year, the celebration will also take place in Rwanda, where our Engineeer4Humanity team has been invited to join World Refugee Day activities coordinated with UNHCR and MINEMA in Kigali and all refugee camps across the country—uniting our global community in one shared message of hope and solidarity.
We warmly invite you to attend, connect, and celebrate with us as we honor refugee achievements and reaffirm our commitment to building a more inclusive and peaceful world.

title: Engineeer4Humanity to Attend Disasters Expo USA – 2026 & 2027
location: George R. Brown Convention Center, Houston TX · Miami, FL
description: Engineeer4Humanity is pleased to announce that Founder & CEO Eric Kamanzi and the Engineeer4Humanity USA Team will participate in the upcoming Disasters Expo USA 2026 (October 14–15, Houston) and Disasters Expo Miami 2027—premier national events focused on disaster mitigation, emergency response, WASH innovation, environmental protection, and resilient-city engineering.
paragraphs: Our participation strengthens the implementation of the MINEMA–UNHCR–engineeer4humanity MoU, especially in Public Health Engineering, WASH, environmental protection, and disaster-risk reduction. The expo also supports Rwanda's national priorities—benefiting Kigali City, refugee-hosting districts, and high-risk areas facing flooding, landslides, wastewater challenges, and environmental degradation.

As a Registered Environmental Firm, and with Eric serving as a lead environmental expert within RAPEP, Engineeer4Humanity uses this platform to bring global solutions home and expand partnerships that improve resilience across Rwanda and Africa.

Strengthening Research, Innovation & University Engagement
The Disasters Expo provides our Research & Innovation Department with:
  • Access to cutting-edge technologies in disaster-risk reduction and WASH
  • Opportunities for joint research with universities and global institutions
  • New models for innovation hubs serving refugee youth and Rwandan students
  • Partnerships that support STEM education, applied engineering, and field research

Advancing Public Health Engineering
The expo directly supports our Public Health Engineering Department by introducing:
  • New solutions for water supply, wastewater treatment, and sanitation
  • Flood-control and erosion-mitigation technologies for Mahama, Mugombwa, Kigeme, and other refugee camps
  • Tools for emergency WASH response and climate-resilient infrastructure
  • Partnerships that strengthen Rwanda's national WASH systems

Empowering Environmental Clubs in Rwanda & Refugee Camps
Environmental Clubs supported by Engineeer4Humanity will benefit through:
  • New ideas for community-led environmental protection
  • Training materials on flood prevention, waste management, and reforestation
  • Youth-friendly tools for climate-action campaigns
  • Opportunities to connect with global environmental networks

Supporting Our Social Enterprise in Rwanda
For Engineeer4Humanity Consultancy Ltd, the expo opens doors to:
  • New environmental technologies for EIA, WASH, and climate-resilience projects
  • Partnerships with U.S. companies seeking to expand into Rwanda & Africa
  • Opportunities to deliver professional engineering services in Rwanda and the region
  • Growth in disaster management, environmental engineering, and resilient-city planning
